title: Wittmackia abbreviata
taxonomy: Wittmackia abbreviata is a species of flowering plant in the family Bromeliaceae. This family is commonly known as the pineapple family. Wittmackia abbreviata is a terrestrial bromeliad, which means it grows on land rather than in a wet environment. Within the family Bromeliaceae, it is classified in the subfamily Tillandsioideae. Wittmackia abbreviata is further classified in the genus Wittmackia.

intro: Wittmackia abbreviata, also known as the Brazilian holly fern, is a species of flowering plant in the family Marantaceae. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can aid in the treatment of wounds and infections. The bioactive constituents of Wittmackia abbreviata include flavonoids, saponins, and tannins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Wittmackia abbreviata is often used to make infusions, decoctions, and ointments, which are commonly used to treat skin conditions, promote wound healing, and reduce inflammation.
